focus: security — SQLite/file trust boundaries, generation pointer validation, corpus and hydration containment, hostile query/vector inputs, exemplar trust, mutation boundaries, and alias recursion

findingDetails.title: The generation pointer accepts a symlinked database outside its publication directory
findingDetails.evidence: Pointer validation constrains only a textual basename, follows an accepted symlink through existsSync/DatabaseSync, and never binds pointer.generationHash to the opened database; a focused probe confirmed pointer_symlink_accepted=true.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The single pointer resolver feeds persistent query and hydration; writer-side staging/rollback validation exists, but exact search and schema tests found no reader-side realpath containment, hash binding, or pointer-symlink test.
findingDetails.claim: A basename-only pointer can resolve through a symlink outside the generation directory and its declared generationHash is not bound to the opened database.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed staged publication and rollback validation at indexer.mjs:1010-1173, searched pointer/symlink tests, and ran focused schema/retrieval suites.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The directory may be writable only by a principal that can already replace generation files.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Prove/enforce an immutable trusted directory for every caller, or add read-side realpath containment and pointer/database generation binding.

findingDetails.title: Persistent retrieval accepts arbitrarily large query vectors
findingDetails.evidence: The request fingerprint serializes the full vector and vectorLane copies it while checking only finite/non-empty values; a 20,000-element focused probe returned ok=true although embedder output is capped at 16,384.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search found the only query-vector validation at retrieval.mjs:210-215; the dimension cap applies only to output vectors, and tests contain no oversized query case.
findingDetails.claim: The synchronous persistent query path accepts and duplicates an unbounded caller-controlled queryVector.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched all queryVector/MAX_VECTOR_DIMENSIONS references and ran retrieval tests; no upstream or query-side cap was found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Current callers may construct small vectors internally rather than expose the API to untrusted network input.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Demonstrate an enforced upstream vector-size cap on every query caller; otherwise validate dimensions and serialized size before fingerprinting.

results.summary: Two security trust/input boundaries fail closed-contract comparison.

evidence: focused probes: pointer symlink and oversized vector accepted
results.summary: Hydration, rights, mutation, and alias claims checked; pointer/vector gaps remain and the full matrix is deferred to traceability.

noveltyJustification: Two new P1 security findings were demonstrated beyond 26 passing focused tests: reader-side pointer symlink acceptance and oversized query-vector acceptance. Prior correctness findings were carried without duplication.
edgeCases: pointer exploitability depends on unrepresented deployment directory permissions
query API is local/in-process with no network exposure found
prompt-injection behavior has static contract evidence but no runtime/model harness
structural-impact analysis unavailable; direct evidence used
first probe reproduced findings but cleanup double-close exited non-zero; corrected probe completed cleanly

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The target combines persistent SQLite publication, filesystem hydration, vector input, and command trust/mutation boundaries.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the security-critical surfaces named by the rendered iteration prompt and phase-003/004 P0 requirements.
omittedHighRiskTargets: runtime/model prompt-injection behavior unavailable in declared target
complete four-phase checklist matrix deferred to traceability

searchLedger.hypothesis: A published pointer cannot redirect readers outside its directory and is bound to its declared generation.

searchActions.queryOrPath: pointer writer/resolver to persistent query and hydration opens
searchActions.result: Writer validates ordinary files; resolver checks basename/exists only and ignores generationHash after shape validation.

searchActions.queryOrPath: basename symlink in temporary database directory

searchLedger.rationale: Reader-side path and generation binding are absent and the escaping target is accepted.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Query vectors are rejected before serialization when they exceed the supported dimension bound.

searchActions.result: The full vector is serialized and copied; only empty/non-finite values are rejected.
The 16,384 cap applies only to embedder outputs; query tests use two dimensions.

searchLedger.rationale: The public synchronous query path has no vector dimension/size bound.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Static traversal, escaping symlinks, stale hashes, or unknown exact-reuse rights can expose corpus content.

searchActions.queryOrPath: index/manifest/hydration realpath, hash, byte, and rights guards
searchActions.result: Containment is checked against corpus/style realpaths; stale hashes and unknown rights refuse.
26/26 passed, including traversal, escaping symlink, stale hash, rights, generation, and byte-cap cases.

searchLedger.rationale: Direct guards and adversarial tests agree.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No contradictory static path was found in the declared corpus/hydration surfaces.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Untrusted exemplars can override workflow authority, advisory commands mutate, or aliases recursively dispatch commands.

searchActions.queryOrPath: shared authority/grounding/mutation contract and canonical/alias wrappers
searchActions.result: Reference material is explicitly untrusted; advisory tools are read-only; aliases execute in current context and prohibit public-command invocation.
9/9 command tests passed, including nested dispatch and silent amendment rejection; prompt-injection resistance remains static contract evidence only.

searchLedger.rationale: Static command and tool boundaries are coherent; runtime/model prompt-injection behavior is unavailable in scope.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No static authority, mutation, or alias-recursion defect was found.

counterevidenceSought: staged publication, rollback validation, schema tests, focused probe
all queryVector/MAX_VECTOR_DIMENSIONS references, retrieval tests, focused probe
